rulings.Case Title: TED J. FOX et al VS. A.W. CHESTER COMPANY et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 13, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT GENERAL DYNAMICS CORPORATION'S Joinder To Defendants Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens. Motion Denied. rulings.Case Title: RICHARD WESTERLIND VS. ALLIED PACKING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Commence Wrongful Death Action Pursuant To C.C.P. § 340.2 And Delay In Prosecution Pursuant To C.C.P. § 583.410-583.420 Filed By Goodyear Tire Rubber Company And Ingersoll-Rand Company
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 13, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANT CELANESE AMERICAS CORPORATION, CELANESE CORPORATION, N/K/A Joinder In Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Commence Wrongful Death Action Pursuant To C.C.P. § 340.2 And Delay In Prosecution Pursuant To C.C.P. § 583.410-583.420 Filed By Goodyear Tire Rubber Company And Ingersoll-Rand Company. DEFENDANT THE GOODYEAR TIRE & RUBBER COMPANY, and, INGERSOLL-RAND COMPANY'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR FAILURE TO PROSCECUTE. The motion is granted under CCP sections 583.410(a) and 583.420(a)(2)(A). Section 340.2(c)(1) does not apply and there exists no law of the case in this action in this court. Movant shall prepare a CRC Rule 3.1312 Order. =(302KOPP/SK)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 13, 2009, line 12, DEFENDANT COSTCO WHOLESALE CORPORATION'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The Court vacates ruling of March 27, 2009 as erroneous. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. =(302KOPP/VC)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN RYAN VS. DIRECTOR OF THE DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Petition For Writ Of Mandate;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 13, 2009, line 13. PETITIONER JOHN RYAN'S Petition For Writ Of Mandate. Petition denied. Petitioner fails to sustain his burden of convincing the court the administrative findings are contrary to the weight of the evidence. Gananian v. Zolin (1995) 33 C. A. 4th 634, 640-641 applies. Weaving constitutes reasonable cause to stop a driver. Officer Fraass' statements comply with Evidence Code section 1280. Documentary evidence shows petitioner was properly informed about the implied consent law. The hearing officer isn't obliged to accept petitioner's version. Excluding his "witness" statements was not error; they were technically irrelevant because none of the persons interviewed by Mr. Keaton was present at the scene of the incident (the "statements" were not affidavits of those persons) (ex. K, Points and Authorities) and Mrs. Fischer's testimony preceded the time of the stop, which she did not observe. Moreover, refusal to undergo a chemical test supervenes any such testimony. The attack on the hearing officer's competency lack convincing merit. =(302/KOPP/PB)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS O'SHEA VS. MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To File Documents Under Seal
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 13, 2009, line 14. RESPONDENT MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO'S MOTION To File Documents Under Seal. Motion granted. =(302/KOPP/JW)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS O'SHEA VS. MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion Requesting The Court'S Signature On/Approval Of Settlement Agreement;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 13, 2009, line 14. PLAINTIFF THOMAS O'SHEA'S Motion Requesting The Court's Signature On/Approval Of Settlement Agreement. Although petitioner disclaims the prescriptions of CCP section 877.6, the Court suppresses its instinct to deny the instant motion and grants the illegitimately-formulated motion at bar so as to obviate further legal expenses for the parties. =(302/KOPP/JW)
